behaviors.description: Request payload to the external API includes the full taxonomy label list on every call.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is request-builder -> API-client handoff (MOCK-MISS), not model judgment. Integration brake: (a) no real OS/network needed if a stub transport captures outbound JSON; (b) correctness does not depend on AI output content; (c) no multi-agent sequence; (d) no LLM-stage orchestration. Rejected higher level because behavior text is about payload inclusion: "request ... includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels."
behaviors.test_description: Wire classifier module with real request-construction code and a stub API endpoint/client that records the sent request. Call classify() with a taxonomy, assert captured payload contains every valid label exactly as configured.

behaviors.description: If API returns a label outside taxonomy, module returns "unknown" to caller.
behaviors.justification: Bug appears at module boundary where raw provider label is validated/normalized before returning (MOCK-MISS). Integration brake: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess required; (b) no dependence on real AI quality since invalid label can be stubbed; (c) no chained agentic steps; (d) no planner/tool/critic handoff. Rejected Agentic/System because behavior text is deterministic mapping of an unexpected value: "returns 'unknown' instead of forwarding."
behaviors.test_description: Use real classifier + normalization pipeline with a stub provider response `{label: "not_in_taxonomy"}`. Assert public classify() result is exactly "unknown".

behaviors.description: Normalization is deterministic for identical raw API responses.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is pure local logic (same input -> same normalized label) with no I/O and no module handoff required. Higher levels are disqualified by text: "identical raw API responses" and "normalization step" describe a single-function/object boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Call normalization function/object repeatedly with the same raw response fixture (including whitespace/case variants encoded in one fixture) and assert identical final label each run.

behaviors.description: Repeated live API calls for same input remain consistent unless provider response changes.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real model/provider behavior across repeated calls (LLM-DEP). A stub cannot reveal drift/nondeterminism, so mocking would hide failure (MOCK-HIDE). Rejected Workflow because this is one agentic decision repeated, not multiple distinct agentic stages in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run N real provider calls for the same document+taxonomy under controlled prompt/settings; record returned labels and raw provider outputs. Assert label changes are only allowed when corresponding provider output changed.

behaviors.description: Token budget policy is enforced before dispatch (truncate/reject per config).
behaviors.justification: Real failure is often config/policy/token-counter/request-builder interaction before send (module boundary). Integration brake: (a) no real OS/network required; (b) does not require model output quality; (c) no multi-agent chain; (d) no LLM-stage orchestration correctness. Rejected System/Agentic because text is explicitly pre-dispatch enforcement: "before dispatch."
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate classifier with real token-budget config and tokenizer/policy wiring; use stub transport to observe whether dispatch occurred. Feed over-budget document and assert configured behavior (truncated payload sent or request rejected) happens before any provider call.

behaviors.description: Provider-reported token usage for typical document stays within configured budget.
behaviors.justification: Needs real external API/token accounting metadata; in-process fakes can mask tokenizer/provider mismatches (REAL-INFRA, MOCK-HIDE). Rejected Agentic because behavior text targets usage telemetry, not model decision quality: "Provider-reported token usage ... within budget."
behaviors.test_description: Execute real API call with representative document and configured budget, read provider-reported usage fields from live response, and assert total billed/request tokens <= configured per-call budget.

behaviors.description: On rate-limit response, module waits and retries instead of failing immediately.
behaviors.justification: Primary failure boundary is retry/backoff orchestration between client wrapper, delay policy, and provider adapter. Integration brake: (a) real OS/network not required if stub returns rate-limit then success; (b) no dependence on AI output content; (c) no multi-agent sequence; (d) no LLM decision handoff. Rejected System/Agentic because behavior text is transport/control-flow: "signals request rate is too high ... waits and retries."
behaviors.test_description: Use real retry middleware/module wiring with stub provider scripted to return rate-limit error then success. Control time via fake clock/sleeper and assert retry attempt count, wait/backoff invocation, and final successful return to caller.
